content:
I had to log in with github account. that uses fcgleason at gmail which I don't like and dont check often.
fcgleason at tgp-architects.com would be better but that is not accepted.
Ah ah, I was able to enable desktop notifications.

The plugin manuals: I think we all agree that the Antora model, where the sources lives in the plugins and is maintained there suits the plugins really well. OTOH, they don't really fit into the Developers Manual (DM), they are end-user info. Also, the arguments for migrating the DM to Antora are stronger and different than for the User Manual (UM).

What if we start thinking in terms of a separate User's Plugin Manual, a third document? Where we can use Antora, without messing with the UM, which we just not are ready for at this point?

Yes! and perhaps it should be built as html for the tarball and offline use.
How should on-line plugin documentation be handled?
What format and where can that be stored and linked to?

I am afraid the Dev Manual has taken some effort and will need considerably more to get it working well. In future, using Dokuwiki and getting the User Manual better organized and streamlined with media files in the right places will help a lot.
